programme.desc: Business coach and passionate foodie Rod is determined to impress. But he's already offended Tanya - and HR manager Claire predicts fireworks..

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Psychic medium Dean plans to put his supernatural skills to good use on his night. The main goes down well - even with Tanya - but his boozy berry cheesecake bombs.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.desc: Disinformation campaigns prove innovative in this investigation of digital deception from Paris to the Philippines. When truth is under attack, democracy is weakened.

programme.title: Creative Types with Virginia Trioli
programme.desc: Trail-blazing comedian Nazeem Hussain wants the "majority to feel like the minority for an hour" with sell-out comedy that breaks all the racial taboos.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: A former Pentecostal preacher, Anthony Venn-Brown struggled with his sexuality as a young man. His coming out and subsequent religious activism has helped reform laws banning gay conversion therapy.

programme.title: ABC Late News

programme.title: The Business

programme.title: Four Corners

programme.title: Media Watch

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Actor, comedian and foodie triple threat Matt Okine drops in. Alice puts her own spin on a festival favourite: the burger; things get messy when Matt gives Alice a hand making hand pulled noodles.

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: News Breakfast
programme.desc: As the day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, interviews and the day's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Nate Byrne with weather.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.

programme.title: Four Corners
programme.desc: Four Corners returns with powerhouse investigative journalism, exposing scandals, triggering inquiries, leading debate and holding power to account for 65 years.

programme.title: Foreign Correspondent
programme.desc: Ireland isn't known for being one of the world's hottest surfing destinations. But in winter surfers head to the village of Mullaghmore, braving the freezing North Atlantic to take on waves as big as multi-storey buildings.

programme.title: Back Roads
programme.desc: Back Roads meets today's sheep shearers. Lisa Millar heads to the Riverina in NSW to join a new breed of shearers, and finds out what it takes to be a competitive sheep shearer.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Ros Childs presents the leading news at lunchtime, including the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

programme.title: National Press Club Address
programme.desc: Martin Parkinson, Chancellor of Macquarie University and former Treasury Secretary, joins Violet Roumeliotis, CEO of SSI, discussing activating Australia's skills for productivity.

programme.title: Media Watch
programme.desc: Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment. Host Linton Besser, a four-time Walkley award-winning investigative reporter and former foreign correspondent, brings you a fearless critique of the week's journalism.

programme.title: Parliament Question Time
programme.desc: The final sitting day in parliament ahead of Easter and the April break. Coverage of Question Time from the House of Representatives. Parliament next sits on Budget Day Tuesday 12 May.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Transformed for today's challenge, a stunning backdrop adorned with a giant gong provides artists with a clue to their semi final celebrity sitter: globally renowned musician and co-founder of Pink Floyd, Nick Mason.

programme.title: QI

programme.desc: When a hedge fund manager who was also running a Ponzi scheme is murdered, Holmes and Watson must determine which of his clients is guilty.
